title: quercus aquatica
intro: Quercus aquatica, also known as the water oak, is a deciduous tree species native to the southeastern United States. The medicinal properties of Quercus aquatica lie in its bark, which contains tannins and other compounds that have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Quercus aquatica include its ability to act as an astringent, reducing inflammation and improving digestion, as well as its potential to help lower cholesterol levels and reduce the risk of heart disease. Quercus aquatica can be prepared for herbal use through infusion, decoction, or tincture, typically made from the bark, leaves, or acorns, although the latter is not commonly used due to its high tannin content.
